title: scenarios v0.3.1 pin bump BLOCKED: 0.3.1 'scenarios hash' CLI switched to block-only canonicalization (ADR-056 D5), diverging from catalog whole-text -> 20 suite failures
description: work_id lead-14xb.1 (request_bugfix, empty scenarios) emitted work_done BLOCKED. Root cause: scenarios 0.3.1 changed the 'scenarios hash' CLI subcommand from whole-text (compute_scenario_hash(stdin)) to parse-then-block-only (strips @-tag lines and Feature: line, ADR-056 D5). The LIBRARY fn scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash is byte-identical v0.2.0..v0.3.1 (so ADR-018 dist-surface analysis called it additive/no-op), but the CLI behavior changed. messaging's catalog ScenarioPayload.hash validator + _canonical_scenario_hash (src/catalog/schemas.py:166) and shop_msg.cli._compute_scenario_hash (cli.py:482) compute WHOLE-TEXT; they agreed with the CLI under 0.2.0 but diverge under 0.3.1 for any body carrying @-tags or a Feature: line. Result: full suite 20 failed / 438 passed under 0.3.1 (458/458 green under 0.2.0). tests/integration/test_catalog_scenarios_agreement.py:65 pins catalog==CLI agreement but its 3 samples are pure Scenario blocks (block-only==whole-text), masking the divergence. RECONCILIATION is product-wide/ADR-level: migrating catalog canonicalization to 0.3.1 block-only changes EVERY on-wire scenario hash for tagged/Feature-bearing bodies; all BCs + lead must adopt simultaneously and the scenario register must be re-pinned. That is scenario-carrying register re-pin work, NOT an empty-scenario pyproject pin bump -> blocked pending lead/architect decision. LIKELY SAME ROOT as bead shopsystem-messaging-90o (bc-emit wrapper block-only recompute 998e5d9596ba9487 vs on-disk whole-text b1fbd8070695aa52): block-only-vs-whole-text canonicalization drift. Downstream impact: bc-base rebuild stays blocked (shop-templates 0.52.0 pins scenarios v0.3.1, messaging pins v0.2.0 -> ResolutionImpossible) until this is reconciled. Two mechanism_observations filed to lead (this + ADR-018 CLI-behavior-blindness).
notes:
ADR-060 (accepted, system-global) answered 6pt's escalation: block-only alignment is CONFORMANCE to ADR-019 D2, NOT new architecture, and needs NO scenario-register re-pin (all 150 pins already block-only). Residual live blocker moved to 5za (lead-authored meta-scenario 17e9342e3cf69969 pins the old whole-text delegation target). bc-base ResolutionImpossible stays blocked until 5za resolves + v0.4.6 ships.
MIGRATION SUBSTANCE RESOLVED (v0.4.6, work_id lead-14xb.1): ADR-060 block-only migration shipped — catalog+cli block-only via scenarios.outstanding.parse_then_block_only_hash, scenarios @v0.3.1, full suite 463 passed, meta-scenario re-pinned 81b972ccea80c8fd. 6pt stays OPEN only because it is dep-blocked by 90o (bc-emit wrapper false-block, independent bug). Close when 90o resolves.

title: bc-emit work-done wrapper false-blocks ALL work_done emits (mis-delimits first-in-file scenario block for abstract_address_registry_adr020.feature)
description: Recurring across lead-d5oj (scenario) and lead-pkq0 (non-scenario release) this session. bc-emit work-done's whole-corpus scenario-hash precondition recomputes 998e5d9596ba9487 for @scenario_hash:b1fbd8070695aa52 (first scenario in features/abstract_address_registry_adr020.feature) and refuses the emit as STALE. The on-disk tag is PROVABLY CORRECT: repo invariant tests/test_scenario_hash_tag_invariant.py passes 151/151, and block-only compute (scenarios hash CLI + compute_scenario_hash) agrees == b1fbd8070695aa52. Root cause: the wrapper's blockifier delimits the FIRST-in-file scenario block differently from shop_msg.cli._build_scenario_payload / ADR-019 (likely including the Feature: line or @-tag lines). Impact: false-blocks every routine work_done emit from this BC until reconciled; both dispatches used the ADR-039 D4 forced-recovery escape valve (--force). Two mechanism_observations filed to the lead (lead-d5oj, lead-pkq0). NOTE: bc-emit tooling lives outside this BC (shop/templates tooling); remediation likely routes through the templates BC, but tracked here for this BC's awareness. Proposed fix: reconcile wrapper block delimitation with _build_scenario_payload; add a regression pinning the wrapper's global scan agrees with test_scenario_hash_tag_invariant on the committed corpus.

Add a _cmd_respond_request_scenario_register handler + 'request_scenario_register' respond subparser mirroring the existing _cmd_respond_request_completion_journal precedent (cli.py:773 / parser cli.py:2715), but building a RequestScenarioRegisterResponse (message_type request_scenario_register_response) whose register_entries is a list[ScenarioRegisterEntry] (hash/title/text/file_location/status), deposited via insert_bc_response into the requester's inbox so it round-trips on read. Recompute the @scenario_hash:2c8501835cf1f5f8 tag with the canonical block-only scenarios hash CLI and confirm equality. Commit as feat(green); the assigned BDD scenario must pass.

title: Test-infra: catalog/shop_msg installed NON-editable -> pytest validates stale code
description: Discovered while draining the lead-4wy..594n batch. 'import catalog' resolves to /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/catalog/ (a non-editable COPY, dir dated today 14:42, no __editable__ link/.egg-link), NOT /workspace/src/catalog/. That installed copy was frozen WITHOUT lead-4wy's WorkId alias (grep -c _WORK_ID_PATTERN|WorkId == 0 installed vs 12 in source). Effect: 'python3 -m pytest tests/' validates against stale installed code that drifts from src/. On origin/main HEAD 9c3b4ed, test_work_id_pattern_symmetry::test_invalid_work_id_rejected_uniformly_by_every_vehicle fails 32/56 with 'DID NOT RAISE ValidationError' even though src is correct; after 'pip install -e .[dev]' it passes 56/56. This also explains the contradictory pass-counts subagents reported (140/274/339) -- install-staleness drift, not just DB order-flakiness. Likely a stray 'pip install .' (non-editable) shadowed the editable install at 14:42.
acceptance_criteria: A conftest/import guard FAILS fast if catalog (and shop_msg) is imported from site-packages instead of /workspace/src (or the suite otherwise pins editable install); test_work_id_pattern_symmetry passes from a clean checkout without a manual reinstall step.
notes: Remediation options: (1) add a conftest.py guard asserting catalog.__file__ and shop_msg.__file__ live under /workspace/src; (2) ensure CI/dev bootstrap uses 'pip install -e' and never bare 'pip install .'; (3) document the reinstall requirement. lead-4wy SOURCE is correct -- this is packaging/test-infra, not a schema bug.

title: Bootstrap shopsystem-messaging as BC shop
description: The messaging repo is missing the BC-shop scaffolding that shopsystem-templates uses as the reference pattern. Currently absent: (1) .claude/agents/bc-implementer.md and .claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md inline subagent definitions; (2) outbox/ directory; (3) BC-shop-specific CLAUDE.md (router pattern, inbox/outbox protocol, what-does-not-happen-here); (4) .gitignore rules for /inbox/ and /outbox/ runtime artifacts. Without these the router can't dispatch bc-implementer/bc-reviewer subagents to process the lead-231.1 and lead-231.2 messages sitting in inbox/. Source the agent files from shopsystem-templates' .claude/agents/ and adapt the BC name and BC root path for messaging.
close_reason: BC-shop scaffolding in place: .claude/agents/{bc-implementer,bc-reviewer}.md adapted from shopsystem-templates with messaging BC name + root path; outbox/ created; .gitignore now ignores /inbox/ and /outbox/ runtime artifacts; CLAUDE.md rewritten as BC-shop variant matching templates' pattern. Test suite (50 tests) green with venv bin on PATH; PATH gap captured as shopsystem-messaging-191.

title: ADR-060 block-only migration blocked by lead-authored meta-scenario 17e9342e3cf69969
description: The ADR-060/ADR-019-D2 block-only canonicalization migration (lead-14xb.1) is correct and lands 462/463 tests green, but full-suite green is unreachable without touching a lead-authored feature pin. Scenario @scenario_hash:17e9342e3cf69969 in features/scenario_hash_block_only_canonical.feature (step 'the shop_msg cli module imports compute_scenario_hash from scenarios.hash', impl tests/conftest.py:11649 then_pw41_imports_in_process) asserts _compute_scenario_hash(@bc-wrapped body) == scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash (whole-text). Under block-only the LHS strips the standalone @bc line (7cfbfe234f153ec8) while whole-text retains it (e260df5d99bcea3a) -> the scenario FAILS. This meta-scenario pins the delegation TARGET itself, which ADR-060 changes; ADR-060's '0 mismatch / no re-pin' analysis covered owned-behavior hash reproduction (invariant still passes for all 150 pins) but not this @origin:adr-019 scenario's now-false step. Clearing it needs the lead/architect to update that scenario's Gherkin + re-pin (or retire it) — a lead authoring decision the dispatch forbids the BC from making. Blocked pending architect direction.
design: RESUME POINTERS: migration already implemented on local branch bc/lead-14xb.1 (NOT pushed) — commits 4cde4a6 test(red) + 7569277 feat(green). Delegation target = scenarios.outstanding.parse_then_block_only_hash (0.3.1; same fn the 0.3.1 'scenarios hash' CLI uses; compute_scenario_hash stays whole-text byte-identical, NOT sufficient). 3 sites migrated: catalog/schemas.py:23 re-export (feeds ScenarioPayload.hash validator :166) + shop_msg/cli.py:120 import + _compute_scenario_hash. RED added to tests/integration/test_catalog_scenarios_agreement.py (tag/Feature-wrapped body, block-only pin 3f123ba774758ff2). Under block-only: full suite 462/463 green; the ONLY red is meta-scenario 17e9342e3cf69969. _editable_guard clean. Product venv restored to scenarios 0.2.0 baseline (151-invariant green). After architect rules on 17e9342e3cf69969 (retire/re-pin), resume from branch bc/lead-14xb.1: rebase on origin/main, finish integrate + v0.4.6 release+tag + artifact verify.
notes: UPDATE (3rd blocked, post-architect-ruling): architect re-authored the meta-scenario to pin 9e9c9ae67254984f with And-line 'imports parse_then_block_only_hash from scenarios.hash'. That pin is UNSATISFIABLE against real scenarios 0.3.1 (v0.3.1, commit fc57a76): scenarios.hash exposes ONLY compute_scenario_hash (whole-text); parse_then_block_only_hash lives ONLY in scenarios.outstanding, NOT re-exported at scenarios.hash -> 'from scenarios.hash import parse_then_block_only_hash' raises ImportError. ROUTER INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED via throwaway venv install of v0.3.1. The hash VALUE 9e9c9ae67254984f is CORRECT (block-only recompute matches via scenarios hash CLI + scenarios.outstanding.parse_then_block_only_hash); only the And-line's import SOURCE is wrong. REMEDIATION (both outside this BC root): (1) shopsystem-scenarios 0.3.x re-exports parse_then_block_only_hash from scenarios.hash, then re-dispatch finishes cleanly; OR (2) lead re-authors the And-line to 'imports parse_then_block_only_hash from scenarios.outstanding' (matches real package + the preserved branch bc/lead-14xb.1 feat(green) which already imports+USES it from scenarios.outstanding). Either unblocks immediately. Migration otherwise complete on branch bc/lead-14xb.1 (462/463 green pre-repin; only blocker was this pin's import source).
close_reason: RESOLVED: architect re-authored meta-scenario And-line to scenarios.outstanding (81b972ccea80c8fd); migration finished + merged 01ace61 + released v0.4.6. Full suite 463 passed against scenarios 0.3.1. work_id lead-14xb.1

title: BLOCKER: bc-base rebuild (publish-bc-base.yml run 29176774669) FAILED at pip ResolutionImpossible — shop-templates 0.52.0 pins scenarios v0.3.1 but shopsystem-messaging (v0.4.4 AND v0.4.5) pins scenar
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-14xb.1
mechanism_observation: ADR-018 contract-surface sufficiency analysis is structurally blind to a dependency's CLI-subcommand behavior change — ADR-018 D1 constrains the lead to analyze a dependency bump from the CONTRACT SURFACE only (installed dist Requires-Dist metadata + git tags), NOT BC source. That surface is structurally blind to a change in a dependency's CLI-SUBCOMMAND behavior. scenarios 0.3.1 moved 'scenarios hash' from whole-text (compute_scenario_hash) to parse-then-block-only canonicalization (ADR-056 D5), yet: (a) Requires-Dist shows only the pin; (b) the library function scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash is BYTE-IDENTICAL across v0.2.0..v0.3.1 (src/scenarios/hash.py diff = empty), so diffing the hashing rule shows nothing; (c) the behavior change lives in cli.py (print(compute_scenario_hash(stdin)) -> print(parse_then_block_only_hash(stdin))), which the contract-surface analysis does not inspect. So the lead's 'additive/no-op, compute_scenario_hash byte-identical' conclusion was a structural FALSE-NEGATIVE of the sufficiency mechanism, not a one-off slip — it mis-scoped a wire-contract-breaking change as an empty-scenario pin bump. Load-bearing for the NEXT dispatch: any future 'bump a scenarios pin' analyzed the same ADR-018-surface way will keep mis-classifying CLI-behavior changes as no-ops.

title: ADR-039 D2 release cadence: cut a VERSION-BUMPED release tag (mandatory per ADR-039 D1 — strictly greater than the lead's installed 0.4.4; a same-version @main reinstall no-ops) and trigger publish, c
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-pkq0
mechanism_observation: bc-emit work-done false-refuses on an unrelated pre-existing stale @scenario_hash, blocking non-scenario release emits — During the v0.4.5 flat release (payload carries zero --scenario-hash), the bc-emit work-done blockifier applied its whole-corpus scenario-hash precondition and false-refused on a single pre-existing stale on-disk tag in an unrelated feature. This makes the wrapper's routine sign-off path unusable for ANY emit from this BC (scenario or not) while that unrelated stale tag exists. Worked around via the documented ADR-039 D4 forced-recovery escape valve (shop-msg respond work_done --force); the underlying release was gate-clean (Check1 clean deliverable tree, Check2 work_id whole-token-reachable on origin/main, Checks 3/5 N/A, Check4 no orphan sub-issues). Minor related spec-slip: acceptance text names 'respond respond_scenario_register' while the landed subcommand (lead-d5oj / e56eab6) is 'respond request_scenario_register', verified against released tag v0.4.5.

title: assign_scenarios from lead (lead-d5oj)
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-d5oj
mechanism_observation: bc-emit work-done wrapper blockifier false-positive-refuses a valid sign-off on an unrelated, correctly-hashed scenario — During reviewer sign-off of lead-d5oj (scenario 2c8501835cf1f5f8, which is faithful, complete, and passes every work-done-gate check), the `bc-emit work-done` wrapper REFUSED the emit with classification STALE, naming an UNRELATED scenario: 'registry add accepts a canonical name with no filesystem path and assigns the abstract address' in features/abstract_address_registry_adr020.feature, tag @scenario_hash:b1fbd8070695aa52, wrapper-recomputed as 998e5d9596ba9487.

That tag is provably CORRECT by the canonical ADR-019 block-only rule. Three independent computations agree the block-only hash of that scenario body == b1fbd8070695aa52 (the on-disk tag): (1) the `scenarios hash` CLI over the scenario block; (2) an in-process call to scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the block-only body; (3) the repo's own authoritative CI invariant tests/test_scenario_hash_tag_invariant.py, which hashes each scenario's canonical body exactly per shop_msg.cli._build_scenario_payload and PASSES 151/151 including this scenario. The wrapper's 998e5d9596ba9487 could not be reproduced by feeding the block-only body, the Feature-line+body, or the tag+Feature+body -- its blockifier is delimiting this (FIRST-in-file) scenario's block differently from the ADR-019 canonical producer, yielding a spurious hash.

Load-bearing: the wrapper's scenario-hash precondition is a GLOBAL scan over all of features/, so a single scenario its blockifier mis-delimits blocks EVERY routine `bc-emit work-done` sign-off in the repo -- including faithful, complete, gate-clean work on an entirely unrelated dispatch. The reviewer had to fall back to the forced-recovery escape valve (bare shop-msg respond work_done --force) to emit a truthful complete, which is exactly the path the design reserves for recovery, not routine sign-off. Until the wrapper's block delimitation is reconciled with shop_msg.cli._build_scenario_payload / test_scenario_hash_tag_invariant, any BC with a scenario of this file-shape cannot use the wrapper for routine sign-offs.

title: BUGFIX: shop-msg send dispatch-side bd write must be a strict additive metadata/notes-only patch on a PRE-EXISTING work_id bead — it must NEVER clobber the bead's identity fields (title/type/priority/
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-yyr9
mechanism_observation: work-done-gate Check 4/Check 1 miss orphaned-duplicate sub-issues + non-durable (uncommitted) bd decomposition — Observed as a recurring Implementer role-discipline failure mode on lead-yyr9: the implementer decomposed twice — an abandoned first pass (3vx.1/.2/.3 left OPEN) and a real TDD-ordered second pass (3vx.4/.5/.6 closed RED->GREEN->refactor) — closing only the second set and never committing .beads/issues.jsonl. The current gate text does not catch this: Check 4 ('>=1 RED sub-issue exists and all sub-issues are closed') is silent on orphaned open duplicates, and Check 1 (clean tree) / Check 4 (closed) are evaluated independently so the failure reads as a generic dirty tree rather than 'the bd decomposition+closures are non-durable / unreachable from origin/main'. Load-bearing for the next BC because abandon-then-recreate under a bd hiccup is a natural Implementer failure mode that will recur. The lead-yyr9 implementation itself is faithful and correct (all scenario/source/teeth probes pass, 444 passed serial); only the bd bookkeeping wedges the gate.

This is load-bearing for lead reconciliation: it risks an unbounded redispatch loop and risks misreading the EXPECTED absence of a literal lead-1v5w commit (order-independent idempotent convergence) as 'work not done', when reachability is transitive via the superseding lead-0udp.

Test-infra: two concurrent plain pytest sessions race the shared shopsystem_test DB drop/create -> spurious mid-run failures
description: lead-ufhs added per-WORKER DB isolation (shopsystem_test_gwN) for xdist, but two independent plain (non-xdist) pytest sessions both target the SAME shopsystem_test base DB and both run pytest_sessionstart DROP/CREATE + pytest_sessionfinish DROP. Running a targeted -k probe alongside a background full-suite run yields false failures like 'database shopsystem_test does not exist' and ~19 failed/87 errored that LOOK like regressions but are pure session-vs-session contention. Observed during lead-ox8 review: a single uncontaminated serial run is 426 passed/0 failed. Remediation candidates: derive the base dbname from PID/session id even outside xdist, or a session lock, so concurrent sessions cannot stomp each other. Relates to c1f (install-staleness) and lead-ufhs (per-worker isolation).

BUG (dispatch-gate / CONTRACT VIOLATION of scenario 48ade065ce073a54). SUPERSEDES the prior 2026-06-09 dispatch under work_id lead-fnj5 (still in the messaging inbox; that one hypothesized a dispatch_
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-r8di
mechanism_observation: lead-r8di gate recurrence was a stale site-packages install-shadow, not a source gate defect — Load-bearing for the lead's q616-family reconciliation. The lead-r8di gate-bug recurrence is NOT a source defect. src/shop_msg/bd_facade.py NON_GATING_DEPENDENCY_TYPES ALREADY includes 'relates-to' (plus related, discovered-from, caused-by, validates, supersedes, tracks), and list_depends_on already excludes those from gating -- so the LIVE-consultation contract (scenario 48ade065ce073a54) was already correctly implemented in source. The real-world violation the lead observed live (reclassify-to-relates-to did NOT clear the gate while full bd dep remove DID) was a STALE site-packages SHADOW: a frozen non-editable copy of shop_msg/catalog under site-packages carrying an OUTDATED NON_GATING set ({parent-child}-only) shadowed the editable /workspace/src checkout, so the running code keyed on the stale set. Same c1f install-shadow failure mode already filed as shopsystem-messaging-c1f, now guarded by the conftest collection-time editable-install guard (origin/main 1fba988 / 71cf840). CONSEQUENCE: lead-r8di shipped ZERO src/ change -- purely additive regression teeth (1 feature file + step defs) that prove the live-consultation contract and would CATCH a re-introduced stale-set regression. If the lead still sees the gate refuse a reclassified-edge send in the field, the diagnosis is a stale install shadow (re-run pip install -e), NOT a source gate defect. The q616-family gate-bug is closed at the contract/source level and was always correct there; the operational recurrence is closed by the editable-install/shadow guard.

BUG: catalog work_id pattern asymmetry across message-type schemas. PRE-STATE (contract/schema surface): Clarify.work_id carries pattern '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$' which REJECTS dots, whereas AssignScenarios.w
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-4wy
mechanism_observation: pytest validates a STALE non-editable catalog install, not src/ — masks lead-4wy on a fresh main checkout — While draining the lead-4wy..594n batch I found that import catalog resolves to /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/catalog/ — a NON-editable COPY (no __editable__ link), frozen WITHOUT lead-4wy's shared WorkId pattern (grep -c of _WORK_ID_PATTERN|WorkId == 0 installed vs 12 in /workspace/src/catalog/schemas.py). Consequence: 'python3 -m pytest tests/' validates against stale installed code that silently drifts from src/. On origin/main HEAD 9c3b4ed, test_work_id_pattern_symmetry::test_invalid_work_id_rejected_uniformly_by_every_vehicle fails 32/56 ('DID NOT RAISE ValidationError') even though the SOURCE fix is correct and reachable; after 'pip install -e .[dev]' the same test passes 56/56. Load-bearing for your reconciliation: re-running the suite on a clean main checkout without an editable reinstall makes lead-4wy (and any catalog-touching work) look regressed when it is not. It also explains the contradictory pass-counts my implementers reported (140/274/339) — install-staleness drift, not just DB order-flakiness. lead-4wy delivery stands; the risk is purely the test harness importing a frozen package.

title: Release shopsystem-messaging v0.2.0. Flat version-bump + git-tag release; no new capability, no scenario changes. Pre-state (verified via gh api over the contract surface, ADR-018 D1): repo carries on
bc-implementer template lacks hash-recompute verification step for @scenario_hash tag edits
description: Observed during lead-5cv (and the lead-231.2 pass that preceded it). The bc-implementer role template does not require the Implementer to recompute the canonical scenario hash via 'scenarios hash' (or scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash) immediately after writing/editing a @scenario_hash:<value> tag on a feature file. In lead-231.2 the Implementer authored mechanism_observation feature files for this BC and wrote fabricated hash values into the tags; the gap was caught only at lead-side reconciliation, costing a full §4.4 round-trip (lead-5cv). The lead's lead-5cv description explicitly flags this: 'a verification step inside the Implementer pass prevents recurrence... the BC may prefer to address it via bc-implementer template tightening.' Load-bearing because: (1) it's a property of the template wording, not of any one work item; (2) the same failure mode applies to any future Implementer pass that authors scenarios (request_bugfix or assign_scenarios with new scenarios, or maintenance that edits scenario tags); (3) the verification is cheap (one CLI invocation per affected file) relative to the round-trip cost. Hypothesis: tighten 'Doing the work — assign_scenarios' and 'Doing the work — request_bugfix' (and add an equivalent clause for request_maintenance when it touches scenario tags) to require: after authoring/editing a @scenario_hash:<value> tag, pipe the file through 'scenarios hash' and confirm the output equals the value just written. Canonical template lives in shopsystem-templates BC.

title: Test runs need PATH to include venv bin so subprocess can find shop-msg
description: tests/conftest.py invokes shop-msg as a bare command via subprocess (e.g. conftest.py:223 in run_send_request_maintenance). When pytest is invoked with the absolute venv python (python3 -m pytest) but without /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin on PATH, the subprocess fails with FileNotFoundError: 'shop-msg'. Workaround: prefix with env PATH="/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin:$PATH". Proper fix: either (a) have conftest.py find shop-msg via shutil.which or sys.executable -m shop_msg, or (b) document the PATH requirement in CLAUDE.md / README. Not a bootstrap blocker — bc-implementer / bc-reviewer subagents use the absolute path /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg per their operating context.
